support_evidence: The Dawn Phase (1915–44): Kinbaku or SM culture in Japan has its origin with Ito Seiu (1882–1961). Ito started his Kinbaku in 1916, if not earlier, and published his Studies on Seme in 1928. In this era, it was essentially a morbid play for crazy people. Ito was often introduced as a nutty man in mass–circulation magazines despite his fame as an artist, theater critic, etc. His Kinbaku was greatly influenced by Kabuki plays, in which princesses, princes or bad guys were often bound. Even in Japanese articles, you may find some that attribute the origins to Hojōjutsu. These are based on false observations. Kinbaku culture in Japan has its roots in theater. But it’s true many modern Kinbakushi subsequently researched Hojōjutsu and incorporated some technique into their Shibari.
